strengths: Completed all major task phases including suture introduction and target passes
Maintained steady instrument control throughout the procedure
Achieved a reasonable completion time of 195 seconds
weaknesses: Final slit closure status could not be verified due to limited visibility
May have insufficient tension or incomplete knot security based on scoring uncertainty
Extracorporeal knot tying technique needs refinement for consistent results

drills.name: Slit-closure check drill
drills.description: Tighten just enough to close the slit without avulsion, then visually inspect and verify complete closure before finishing.

drills.name: Knot-pusher sequencing drill
drills.description: Practice 10 three-throw extracorporeal sequences with deliberate pusher advancement and consistent tension control.

drills.name: Mark-to-mark needle path drill
drills.description: Focus on 10 precise passes through both targets before any knot tying to ensure optimal suture placement.

progression_note: At the intermediate level, focus on converting your consistent completion into reliable slit closure with proper knot security. Work on final inspection techniques to ensure task completion meets all criteria.
